title: Mirjan Fort
tagline: Mirjan Fort — heritage city of Karnataka

overview.short: The Mirjan Fort is located on the west coast of the Uttara Kannada district in the southern Indian state of Karnataka.
overview.description: The Mirjan Fort is located on the west coast of the Uttara Kannada district in the southern Indian state of Karnataka. The fort known for its architectural elegance has been the location of several battles in the past. It is about 0.5 kilometres (0.31 mi) from the National Highway 66 and 21 kilometres (13 mi) from Gokarna, the famous Hindu pilgrimage centre on the west coast of India. According to the first historical version, Rani Chennabhairadevi of Gersoppa (under the Vijayanagara Empire) was initially credited with building the Mirjan Fort in the 16th century.

topPlaces.name: Aigalkurve
topPlaces.description: Aigalkurve is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Kumta taluk of Uttara Kannada district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Hegde (village)
topPlaces.description: Hegde is a village in Kumta on the western coast of India in the Uttara Kannada district of the state of Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Halkar
topPlaces.description: Halkar is a village in Kumta Taluk, Uttara Kannada, Karnataka, India.

topPlaces.name: Holanagadde
topPlaces.description: Holanagadde is a village in the Uttara Kannada district of the state of Karnataka, India. It is located on the west coast beach of the Arabian Sea.

topPlaces.name: Aghanashini (village)
topPlaces.description: Aghanashini (transl. One who destroys sins) is a small village situated on the southern banks of River Aghanashini in the state of Karnataka, India. The river Aghanashini originates at 'Shankara Honda' in the Sirsi city.

topPlaces.name: Kumta
topPlaces.description: Kumta is a town and taluk in the Uttara Kannada district of Karnataka, India. It is one of the important stations along the Konkan Railway line running between Mumbai and Mangalore.

topPlaces.name: Gonehalli
topPlaces.description: Gonehalli is a small village in Kumta taluka. Its neighboring villages are Torke, Devarbhavi and Bankikodla.

topPlaces.name: Devarbhavi
topPlaces.description: Devarbhavi is a village in Kumta Taluka. It is next to Torke village.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Mirjan Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Mirjan Fort is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Mirjan Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Goa International Airport, Dabolim (117 km) and the nearest railway station is Madgaon Junction (Goa) (100 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Mirjan Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Mirjan Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Mirjan Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Mirjan Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Mirjan Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Aigalkurve, Hegde (village), Halkar, Holanagadde, Aghanashini (village).
